symptom: Avere cache exhibits slot exhaustion, recycling by space, and elevated client-facing latency
rootCause: Write-heavy workload running against a read-only (or bypass) cache policy; cache cannot absorb writes, causing slot exhaustion and continuous recycling
solution: Match cache policy to workload type: use writeback or readwrite policy for write-heavy workloads. Read-only policy is only appropriate for ~80% read / 20% write workloads. Verify policy in cluster dashboard and update via Avere Control Panel
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Avere vFXT Quick Reference Sheet

symptom: Long-running revocations or stuck NFS operations on Avere cluster that do not self-resolve
rootCause: A service is stuck on the specific node that first raised the revocation condition, holding locks and preventing operation completion across the cluster
solution: Identify the node that first raised the condition (check dashboard alerts/conditions for the originating node). Restart services on that specific node to release stuck operations. Use 'averecmd alert.clearAllConditions' after restart to clear stale conditions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Avere vFXT Quick Reference Sheet

symptom: Avere cluster nodes showing progressive swap pressure, memory drift, or unexplained performance degradation over time
rootCause: Very long node uptimes (weeks/months) lead to memory pressure (swap usage increasing) and accumulated state drift in cluster services
solution: Plan periodic reboots as routine hygiene. HA-mode reboot: orchestrated, minimal disruption but slower overall. Simultaneous reboot: faster with brief global disruption, schedule in a maintenance window. Monitor swap via 'top' or 'cluster exec.py uptime'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Avere vFXT Quick Reference Sheet

symptom: Avere dashboard metrics appear delayed or do not reflect recent cluster activity / changes
rootCause: Dashboard has a built-in ~5 minute display lag; stats are aggregated in 10-minute windows, making real-time troubleshooting on dashboard alone unreliable
solution: Account for 5-minute dashboard lag and 10-minute stat aggregation windows when correlating metrics. For real-time data use GSI/support bundle instead. Ensure host and cluster clocks are UTC-aligned before comparing windows. Run 'date' on both host and cluster to verify
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Avere vFXT Quick Reference Sheet

symptom: Avere 集群出现高客户端延迟，与 core filer 延迟同步上升；nfs_back_proc 统计显示 req.hunt、req.asyncappend 显著增大，cwndlimit 持续递增；recv.timerExpire = 0
rootCause: RPC slot exhaustion：集群节点到 core filer 的 TCP 连接 RPC 队列已满（默认 256 slots/连接，可能受 core filer 限制降至 16/32/64），大量 NFS 操作因无法入队而阻塞
solution: (1) 优先方案：增加 TCP 连接倍增器，通过自定义设置 massX.nfsConnMult（默认 4，建议增至 8；最大值 23，通常不超过 16）。应用后等待延迟下降，再获取 Support Bundle 验证。(2) 备选方案：增加集群 IP 数量和每节点 IP 数量，以创建更多 TCP 连接。注意：变更前须确认 core filer 负载 < 80%，并排除网络拥塞；需经 Escalation 或 Management 批准
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Troubleshooting/RPC Slot Exhaustion - NFS

symptom: Avere 集群高客户端延迟，且 nfs_back_proc 中 recv.timerExpire > 0；core filer 在规定超时内未响应 NFS 请求（cluster.NfsBackEndTimeout ≥ 55s）
rootCause: 非 RPC slot exhaustion：NFS 调用超时等待 core filer 响应，可能是 core filer 响应慢或网络问题导致请求在 backend 超时
solution: 排除 RPC slot exhaustion，进行数据包抓包（packet capture）分析 + 深入 stats 调查；检查 core filer 健康状态和网络链路质量
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Troubleshooting/RPC Slot Exhaustion - NFS

symptom: Kubernetes pod keeps restarting repeatedly (CrashLoopBackOff) on Arc-enabled cluster
rootCause: Application failure or container misconfiguration — bad entrypoint, missing environment variables, or crashing process in container
solution: 1) kubectl get pods -n <namespace> to identify failing pod; 2) kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n <namespace> for Events; 3) kubectl logs <pod-name> -n <namespace> --previous to inspect crash output and trace root cause
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Container Storage Enabled by Azure Arc/Common errors Kubernetes

symptom: Kubernetes pods remain in Pending state and cannot be scheduled on Arc-enabled cluster
rootCause: Resource constraints (insufficient CPU/memory on nodes), unbound PersistentVolumeClaims, or node taint/toleration mismatch preventing scheduling
solution: kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n <namespace> to check FailedScheduling events; kubectl get nodes + kubectl describe node <node-name> to verify node capacity, taints, and availability
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Container Storage Enabled by Azure Arc/Common errors Kubernetes

symptom: Container fails to start with CreateContainerConfigError status in Kubernetes pod on Arc-enabled cluster
rootCause: Missing or incorrect ConfigMap/Secret reference, volumeMount/subPath mismatch, or expected path missing on node
solution: 1) kubectl get pods -A | grep -i CreateContainerConfigError; 2) kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n <namespace> for Events; 3) kubectl get events -n <namespace> --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -50. Look for 'configmap not found', 'secret not found', subPath/volumeMount errors, or 'no such file or directory'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Container Storage Enabled by Azure Arc/Common errors Kubernetes

symptom: Azure Data Box Disk (NTFS-formatted) not mounted or accessible on Linux after rsync process was killed or system was restarted during data copy
rootCause: NTFS volume inconsistencies caused by unclean unmount; NTFS journal file needs reset after abrupt process termination or reboot
solution: Run `ntfsfix /dev/sdn` (replace sdn with actual device name) to fix NTFS inconsistencies and reset the journal file, then retry mounting the disk and resuming the copy job
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Connect & Copy/Azure Databox Disk is not mounted or accessible after process is killed or system is restarted

symptom: Data uploaded from Azure Data Box Disk ends up in the $root container instead of the intended container in Azure Storage account
rootCause: Container structure in Azure Storage is determined by subfolder names under BlockBlob/PageBlob on the disk. Files placed directly under BlockBlob or PageBlob (not in a subfolder) are uploaded to the default $root container
solution: Create named subfolders under BlockBlob or PageBlob on the Data Box Disk matching the target container names. Structure: <BlockBlob>/<container-name>/<files>. The Data Box upload process will create/use containers matching those subfolder names in Azure Storage.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Connect & Copy/How is data from the Azure Data Box Disk uploaded to a container in the storage account?

symptom: Customer requests private endpoint or private networking for ACSA Cloud Ingest traffic to Azure Storage
rootCause: Azure Container Storage enabled by Azure Arc (ACSA) Cloud Ingest does not support private endpoints or private networking as of current release
solution: Not supported. Cloud Ingest data currently relies on public endpoints of Azure Storage. Inform customer of current limitation; no workaround available. Monitor for future GA announcements.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Container Storage Enabled by Azure Arc/FAQ Frequenly asked questions

symptom: Unable to mount Azure Data Box Disk on Oracle Linux version 7.9; OS is not recognized as compatible with the unlock/mount tooling.
rootCause: Oracle Linux Server 7.9 is not a supported operating system for Azure Data Box Disk. Only OS versions listed in the official Azure Data Box Disk system requirements are supported; newer untested versions may work but are not guaranteed.
solution: Switch to a supported OS such as CentOS. Refer to the Azure Data Box Disk system requirements documentation (https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databox/data-box-disk-system-requirements) for the full supported OS list.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Connect & Copy/Unable to mount Azure Data Box Disk using Oracle Linux version 7.9

symptom: Azure Data Box Disk enters 'Security Locked' state at the datacenter; all reads and writes fail; disk is detected as RAID-visible but not OS-visible; CreateDCInfra fails with DiskSecurityLockedException message.
rootCause: Abnormal rapid power drops in quick succession (e.g., sudden disk removal or insufficient power from USB hub) cause the disk firmware to enter a self-protective 'Security Locked' state. This is unrelated to BitLocker volume lock state and does not indicate a data security breach.
solution: The disk cannot be recovered once in Security Locked state. Perform a secure erase on the disk. Customer must create a new Data Box Disk order and redo the entire import process.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Data Copy at Azure/Azure DC cannot upload the data from the Data Box Disk because the Azure Data Box Disk is in security locked state

symptom: Blob upload fails at Azure datacenter with error code 'IOFailed'; this error code does not appear in public Azure documentation.
rootCause: Hardware failure on the Data Box Disk causes a disk read error during the datacenter upload process. This is a hardware defect and is not preventable by the customer.
solution: 1) Run the DataBoxDiskValidation tool before shipping to verify data integrity. 2) If validation shows errors, order another Data Box Disk and re-upload the failing files. 3) Alternatively, manually upload failing files via Azure Portal, AzCopy, or Azure Storage Explorer. 4) For future large transfers, consider using Data Box Pod (RAID 5, more resilient to single-disk failures).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Data Copy at Azure/Blob upload failed, error code 'IOFailed', not seen in public documentation

symptom: Data Box Disk upload to Azure at the datacenter is stuck/frozen at the same 'GB copied' value for multiple days (e.g., >4 days for 6TB). ASC job details show EntityHealthState: 'Faulted', Reason: 'DriveCopyTimeout'.
rootCause: Drive Copy Timeout caused by DNS resolution failure between the datacenter device, server, and storage account ('The Remote name could not be resolved'). An auto-generated WatchDog IcM is usually already created.
solution: 1) Check ASC job details for Upload Progress section (verify EntityHealthState and Reason fields). 2) Check for an existing auto-generated WatchDog IcM; contact the IcM owner with ASC analysis. 3) If no resolution, create a new IcM with the Data Box Data Path Engineering Team. 4) The DC engineer will reload the disk onto another server to re-attempt the copy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Data Copy at Azure/Copy to Azure is stuck at Azure Data Box Disk offload

symptom: Data Box Disk shows 'Internal Error' in the Azure Portal after the device has been returned to the datacenter; customer cannot view logs or track progress.
rootCause: Upload or processing issue at the datacenter; specific root cause requires investigation via ASC Resource Explorer and the Data Transfer Status tab.
solution: 1) Go to ASC and open Resource Explorer for the order. 2) Check the Data Transfer Status tab for copy status details. 3) Check the Active ICM tab for any existing incidents. 4) If an ICM exists, monitor it and keep the customer informed. 5) If no ICM exists, create one with the Data Box Data Path team for investigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Data Copy at Azure/Data Box disk showing as Internal Error in Azure Portal

symptom: Data copy to Azure Files from Data Box Disk completes with status 'CompletedWithErrors' and/or 'BadRequest'; specific files fail to upload due to invalid file names (e.g., names containing XML-like special characters such as '<FileStoragePathEncoding="Base64_UTF32">').
rootCause: File names, directory names, or share names do not comply with Azure Files naming conventions. The DataBoxDiskValidation.cmd tool cannot catch all invalid file name scenarios, so some invalid files pass validation but fail during the actual upload.
solution: 1) Verify all shares, directories, and file names follow Azure naming conventions (https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/storageservices/naming-and-referencing-shares--directories--files--and-metadata). 2) Check Drive:\DataBoxDiskImport\logs\error.xml for failed entries. 3) Decode and fix the file names per Azure conventions. 4) Use AzCopy to manually re-upload the corrected files: `azcopy copy '<local-file-path>' 'https://<storage-account>.file.core.windows.net/<file-share>/<file-name><SAS-token>'`
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Data Copy at Azure/Data Copy to Azure Files Completed with 'CompletedWithErrors' and-or 'BadRequest' Status

symptom: Customer returned Data Box Disk using their own shipping label (not the Microsoft-provided one); the Azure portal shows no updates or progress on the Data Box order/job after the device was physically received at the datacenter.
rootCause: When the return is shipped with a non-Microsoft-provided label, the shipment is not automatically linked to the Data Box order in the system. The datacenter cannot initiate the data transfer job without this linkage.
solution: 1) Obtain the customer's tracking number and shipment details. 2) Create an IcM with the Data Box Operations Team, including order details and tracking info. 3) The Operations team will notify the Datacenter to manually locate the package and start the data transfer job. 4) Advise customer to always inform Microsoft Support before using their own shipping label in future shipments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Device Return - Logistics/The customer has returned the device with another label and has not received any updates on Data Box Disk order

symptom: Generic 'Access Denied' error when running the Data Box Disk Validation Tool (checksum or Prepare to Ship phase); multiple possible causes including permission issues, folder attributes, or data type.
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-generic-access-denied-validation-tool.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Disk Validation/Generic "Access Denied" Error When Using Data Box Disk Validation Tool

symptom: Subfolders created inside the AzureFile folder on Data Box Disk appear to disappear or go missing during the Validation Tool run, even though Robocopy completed successfully with no errors.
rootCause: The subfolders are not actually deleted; they are either hidden (hidden attribute set) or the Validation Tool/user context lacks sufficient permissions to view them.
solution: Run `dir Drive:\AzureFile` from a Command Prompt to verify whether the subfolders are present. If they appear in the dir output, they are hidden or permission-restricted; address permissions (e.g., grant appropriate access) or remove the hidden attribute as needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Disk Validation/Subfolders created inside the AzureFile folder disappear on Azure Data Box Disk during Validation

symptom: Data Box Disk shows 'Drive Formatted' error at the datacenter; ASC copy status shows 'Bitlocker Failed'; disk is invalid for data transfer after customer formatted it upon arrival.
rootCause: Customer or vendor technician formatted the Data Box Disk after receiving it, erasing the pre-configured disk setup and the BitLocker encryption set by Microsoft. The disk then becomes unusable for the intended import process because the datacenter cannot recognize it.
solution: There is no recovery for a formatted Data Box Disk (customer error). If multiple disks were ordered and others remain unformatted, those can still be used. Customer must place a new Data Box Disk order and follow the correct procedure: do NOT format the disk; use the provided BitLocker key and documentation to unlock and access the disk.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Hardware & Unlock/"Drive Formatted" Error on Azure Data Box Disk

symptom: During data copy to Azure Data Box Disk, error 'Power Surge On Hub Port: A USB device has exceeded the power limits of its hub port' appears and USB connection terminates
rootCause: USB device draws more power than the hub port allows; some systems (e.g., Data Loggers based on Windows Embedded OS) share power between USB ports, limiting available power per port
solution: (1) Confirm client OS is supported and meets system requirements; (2) Verify USB cable is properly connected; (3) Connect Data Box Disk directly to client machine — remove all USB hubs, adapters, and third-party devices; (4) Reproduce on different host machines to isolate host-specific issue; (5) If unresolved, engage Engineering via ICM to DataBox\Data path team for alternate power adapter that draws from 240V adapter instead of USB port
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Hardware & Unlock/Error 'Power Surge On Hub Port: A USB device has exceeded the power limits of its hub port' During Data Copy for Azure Data Box Disk

symptom: After unlocking Azure Data Box Disk, copying data fails with 'ERROR: The media is write protected'; disk contents are visible but no data can be written
rootCause: Disk read-only attribute may be set; could also be caused by host write policy, insufficient folder permissions, or antivirus interference
solution: (1) Try drag-and-drop instead of Robocopy to rule out app-specific issues; (2) Test on multiple host devices to rule out host policy; (3) Connect another USB disk to confirm host R/W capability; (4) Ensure running with admin account; (5) Check and enable write permissions on disk folder; (6) Take ownership of folders; (7) Disable antivirus software; (8) Run diskpart as admin: list disk → select disk N → attributes disk clear readonly; (9) If unresolved, create ICM for Data Box Data Path engineering team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Hardware & Unlock/The Media is Write Protected error on Azure Data Box Disk

symptom: Unlocking Data Box Disk on RHEL 7.x host fails with 'passkey is wrong' error; logs show '[ERROR]: Decryption failed as dislocker-file was not created'
rootCause: DataBoxDiskUnlock_Prep.sh contains a broken URL (HTTP 404) at line 400 (wget http://dl.fedoraproject.org/pub/epel/7/x86_64/Packages/e/epel-release-7-11.noarch.rpm), causing required BitLocker unlock package dependencies to fail installation
solution: (1) Ensure customer has latest version of prep script; (2) If issue persists, manually download epel-release: wget https://dl.fedoraproject.org/pub/epel/epel-release-latest-7.noarch.rpm; (3) Install: yum install ./epel-release-latest-7.noarch.rpm; (4) Re-run DataBoxDiskUnlock_Prep.sh — required packages should now install successfully
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Hardware & Unlock/Unlocking a Data Box Disk on Red Hat Linux (RHEL) 7.x host fails with an error saying that the passkey is wrong

symptom: Azure Data Box Disk is undetected by host machine or detected but cannot be unlocked despite following documentation
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-usbtreeview-troubleshoot-undetected-disk-unlocking.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Hardware & Unlock/Using USBTreeView tool to troubleshoot undetected disks or disks that cannot be unlocked

symptom: Customer wants to change the storage account associated with an existing Data Box order after order has been processed
rootCause: Storage account is locked and cannot be changed once the order is processed by design
solution: Storage account cannot be changed after order processing; customer must: (1) Return the shipped Data Box device; (2) Create a new order specifying the correct storage account
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Ordering & Logistics/Data Box Disk FAQs

symptom: Customer cannot find their country in the source country drop-down when creating a Data Box order in Azure portal
rootCause: Data Box service is not available in the customer's source country; unavailable countries are excluded from the drop-down
solution: Workaround options: (1) Use a branch office located in a supported country to place the order and transport the device manually (customer is responsible once transported); (2) Use self-managed shipping — provide a supported country address, customer picks up the Data Box from the datacenter, copies data, then returns it to the datacenter. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databox/data-box-disk-portal-customer-managed-shipping
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Disk/Ordering & Logistics/Data Box Disk FAQs

symptom: Customer wants to expand storage capacity on Data Box Gateway virtual device; does not know correct procedure or tries to expand default data drive
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-expand-data-disk-data-box-gateway.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Gateway/Configure & Setup/Process to Expand Data Disk on Data Box Gateway

symptom: Data Box Gateway device sends data to local cache but does not upload to Azure cloud; Azure portal shows warning 'Could not upload <N> file(s) from share <share-name>'
rootCause: Destination blobs in Azure Storage have been set to 'Archive' access tier (at blob level, not container/account level), typically triggered by a Blob Lifecycle Management policy; archived blobs return HTTP 409 'This operation is not permitted on an archived blob' (eec=BlobArchived)
solution: (1) Check ASC or Azure portal for Lifecycle Management policy: Storage Account → Blob service → Lifecycle Management; (2) Confirm in device logs: Get-HcsAlerts (FileUploadFailureCondition) and hcsedgeagentwriter logs (BlobArchived s=409); (3) Rehydrate archived blobs via: (a) manual portal/CLI per blob, (b) modify/delete lifecycle policy to rehydrate to Cool/Hot tier, or (c) PowerShell script to bulk set blobs to Hot tier; (4) Verify diagnostics tests pass in local WebUI (Troubleshooting → Diagnostics Tests); (5) High-priority rehydration available (preview) but incurs higher cost
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Gateway/Connectivity & Performance/Data is not being uploaded to the Azure cloud on Data Box Gateway

symptom: Error 0x8007003A when copying files from Data Box Gateway share to local machine
rootCause: Associated blobs in Azure Storage have been archived; Data Box Gateway cannot read archived blobs; hcsedgeagentreader logs show 'This operation is not permitted on an archived blob.' s=409 eec='BlobArchived'
solution: Rehydrate archived blobs from Archive tier to Cool or Hot tier; refer to Blob rehydration from archive tier documentation (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/archive-rehydrate-overview); may require collaboration with Blob Storage support team via collab case
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Gateway/Connectivity & Performance/Getting Error 0x8007003A while copying files from Gateway share to local machine

symptom: Files in Data Box Gateway share folder open slowly; customer wants to identify which files reside in local device cache vs. Azure storage
rootCause: By design, once the device reaches a storage threshold, files are tiered to Azure (local data removed, only metadata retained); files with no local data must be fetched from Azure on first access, causing slow open times
solution: (1) In File Explorer, right-click file → Properties → compare 'Size' vs 'Size on Disk': if Size on Disk = 0, file resides only in Azure (slow first open); (2) Use PowerShell to bulk-check size on disk across the share; (3) Once a file is opened once, it will be locally cached and subsequent opens will be faster
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Gateway/Connectivity & Performance/Identifying which files are on the Local Cache (VM Data Disk) of Data Box Gateway

symptom: Azure Data Box Gateway share status permanently shows 'Updating' in Azure Portal; last updated time and error logs are blank
rootCause: Error 2002: files on the share are currently open/locked by applications and cannot be uploaded to cloud until they are closed
solution: Close all open files on the affected share to allow the Data Box Gateway writer to retry and complete the upload
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Gateway/Connectivity & Performance/The status of DBG Share is always shown in 'updating' state in Azure Portal

symptom: Upload failure with error 2003 on Azure Data Box Gateway; files fail to upload from a share whose root datadata folder has ReadOnly directory attribute set
rootCause: The share root or datadata folder has the ReadOnly attribute set, preventing the HCS edge agent writer from uploading files. This can occur when robocopy is used without the /nodcopy flag, causing it to copy directory attributes including ReadOnly
solution: Create a non-readonly empty folder locally and robocopy it to the share root to clear the ReadOnly attribute. For future data copies, use robocopy with the /nodcopy flag to avoid copying directory attributes to the DataBox share
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Gateway/Connectivity & Performance/Unable to upload files on DBG due to readonly being set on the share's 'datadata' folder

symptom: Upload error 2003 in Azure Data Box Gateway upload logs (nonTransientErrors); files in certain directories cannot be accessed or uploaded by the appliance
rootCause: Directory or file names end with a space or period. While the underlying file system may support such names, the Windows shell and UI cannot access them, causing upload failure
solution: Move the affected files into directories with names that do not end with a space or period. After moving, the appliance will successfully upload the files. Note: using a period as the first character (e.g., .temp) is acceptable
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box Gateway/Connectivity & Performance/Upload Error 2003 on Azure Data Box Gateway

symptom: Azure Data Box copy job is stuck and not progressing; hcsdpservice.Primary logs show 'Directory open failed with err: 5' and 'NativeLog Enumeration of endpoint failed with error: 5'
rootCause: Windows is blocking reads by local users over the network (error 5 = Access Denied); the user or process performing the copy lacks sufficient read permissions on the source folders
solution: Option 1: Manually grant the copy user read permissions on affected folders via folder Properties > Security > Edit > Add. Option 2: Incrementally lower UAC (User Account Control) settings via Windows Search > 'Change User Account Control settings' until the copy job progresses. Run diagnostics in the device UI (Troubleshooting > Diagnostic test) first to rule out other issues
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Data Box Copy Job is not Progressing

symptom: Files accidentally copied to wrong share folder on DataBox device (e.g., to _AzFiles instead of _BlockBlob); moving via Windows File Explorer triggers a slow copy operation
rootCause: Moving files between DataBox shares using File Explorer initiates a cross-share copy rather than a true move operation, which can be extremely time-consuming for large datasets
solution: Use PowerShell Move-Item command on Windows: Move-Item -Path <SourcePath> -Destination <DestinationPath>. On Linux, use the mv command. Connect to the DataBox share path (e.g., \\DataBoxIPAddress\StorageAccountName_Blockblob) first. This is significantly faster than re-copying
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Moving files on Data Box device without having to re-copy

symptom: Multiple users or clients receive error 1909 'The referenced account is currently locked out and may not be logged on to' when connecting to Azure Data Box via net use or SMB
rootCause: The Data Box device user account is locked out after 5 consecutive invalid login attempts. Commonly triggered by scripts/programmatic access, multiple simultaneous clients, or third-party tools (e.g., Commvault) using wrong credentials or cached incorrect passwords
solution: 1) Wait 40 minutes for the account to auto-unlock, then reconnect from a single client manually using correct credentials. 2) If recurring, collect a support bundle and check Security.evtx event logs for EventID 4625 (failed logon), 4776 (credential validation), 4740 (account lockout), 4767 (account unlock), 4725 (account disabled) to identify the offending client. 3) Temporarily disable or isolate the offending client to stop the lockout cycle
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Multiple users trying to access Data Box and getting locked out error 1909

symptom: Customer does not want to retain source ACLs when copying data to Data Box; wants files to inherit destination domain permissions instead of source domain ACLs
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-applying-acl-permissions-data-box.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Applying ACL and Permissions at destination and removing source ACLs before data transfer to Data Box

symptom: Data copy to Azure Data Box is very slow; multiple possible causes including millions of small files, slow host machine, or third-party filter drivers
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-data-copy-slow-data-box.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Data Copy very slow when copying to Data Box

symptom: After Data Box data transfer, metadata such as ACLs, timestamps, or file attributes are not preserved; or users encounter permission errors editing/deleting transferred data
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-metadata-preservation-permissions-data-box.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Metadata Preservation and Permissions Issues on Data Box

symptom: 向 Data Box NFS 共享写入数据时，chown 操作返回 AccessDenied 错误；Cohesity 等第三方工具因需要 ownership 而无法直接写入 Data Box NFS 共享/文件夹
rootCause: Data Box 不支持在 NFS 共享/文件夹上授予 ownership 权限，此为设计行为（by design）
solution: 先在 Linux 主机上挂载 NFS 共享，用 mkdir -p 创建目录并 chmod -R 777 设置权限，再将该目录添加到 Cohesity/第三方工具；配置 Data Box WebUI NFS 客户端访问 IP 白名单（Connect and copy > NFS settings > NFS client access）
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Permission error when writing to Data Box via NFS

symptom: 使用 Azure Data Box 配合 MARS Agent 进行离线备份到 Recovery Services Vault 时，MARS Agent 无法检测到 NFS 共享路径
rootCause: Windows 客户端未启用 NFS 客户端功能，或 NFS 客户端 IP 未添加到 Data Box 本地 WebUI 的 NFS 允许客户端 IP 列表中；文档仅提及 Linux NFS，但 Windows 下 MARS Agent 同样需要 NFS 客户端
solution: 1) 确保宿主机已启用 NFS 客户端 Windows 功能；2) 将 Data Box 挂载为 Local System；3) 在 Data Box 本地 WebUI 的 Connect & Copy > NFS > Allowed Client IP Addresses 中添加 NFS 客户端 IP
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Problem detecting NFS share on Data Box being used with Microsoft Recovery Services Vault

symptom: Azure Data Box SMB 共享凭据在 45 天后过期，导致客户端无法访问 SMB 共享
rootCause: SMB 凭据默认过期时间为 45 天，配置时未将密码设置为永不过期
solution: 通过 MiniShell 连接到 Data Box（PowerShell PSSession via storsimpleadmin，Enable-HcsSupportAccess，再 Enter-PSSession via StorSimpleSupport/SupportSession），执行 net user /add 添加用户，Grant-SmbShareAccess 授予 SMB 共享权限，最后用 WMIC USERACCOUNT WHERE Name=... SET PasswordExpires=FALSE 将密码设为永不过期
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/SMB Password Reset on Azure Data Box Share

symptom: Linux 主机与 Azure Data Box Pod 之间 NFS 传输速率过慢
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-Slow-NFS-Transfer-Rate-Linux-DataBox.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Slow NFS Transfer Rate between Linux Servers and Data Box

symptom: 无法访问 Azure Data Box 本地 Web UI，浏览器连接失败
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-Unable-to-Access-DataBox-Local-WebUI.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to Access Data Box Local Web UI

symptom: 客户端无法访问 Azure Data Box/Stack Edge/Data Box Gateway SMB 共享；Smbserver.Security 日志显示 'LmCompatibilityLevel value is different from the default'（配置值=5，默认值=3）或 Session Authentication Failure 0xC000006D
rootCause: 客户端与 Data Box 之间 NTLM LmCompatibilityLevel 设置不兼容；Data Box 要求 NTLMv2（Level 5），而客户端配置了较低级别（如 0-3），导致身份验证协商失败
solution: 在客户端运行 secpol.msc → Local Policies > Security Options > Network Security: LAN Manager authentication level，设置为 'Send NTLMv2 response only/refuse LM and NTLM'；或通过 regedit 修改 HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA\LMCompatibilityLevel = 5，重启生效
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to access data box share Permissions - NTLM configuration issues

symptom: 使用 robocopy /B /MIR /IT /COPY:DATSO /DCOPY:DAT 保留 ACL 将数据复制到 Data Box 后，通过 File Explorer 无法浏览或访问 Data Box SMB 共享上的文件夹，出现权限错误且无法查看 ACL
rootCause: Data Box 未加入域，无法识别源服务器的域用户 ID/安全上下文；File Explorer 不支持 backup semantics，而有限制性 ACL 的文件夹需要 backup semantics 才能访问
solution: 使用支持 backup semantics 的工具（如 robocopy /B）访问数据；验证 ACL 可使用内部 ACL Compare Tool（\\hcsfs\scratch\ramgana\ACLCopyTool\DVTool.exe --mode acl_compare）；或通过 support session 临时禁用 ACL 强制执行（需客户明确同意并升级 TA/工程团队跟踪）；数据已上传到存储账户则超出 Data Box 支持范围，需联系 Azure Files 团队
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to access folders and files on Data Box after preserving ACLs

symptom: 客户端无法连接到 Data Box SMB 或 NFS 共享（多种错误：网络路径未找到、无权访问、系统找不到指定文件、3rd party filter driver 干扰）
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-Unable-to-Connect-DataBox-SMB-NFS-Shares.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to connect to the Databox SMB or NFS shares over the network

symptom: 使用 Azure Storage Explorer 连接 Data Box 并创建容器时报错：'Failed to create Blob container getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND *.blob.*.microsoftdatabox.com'
rootCause: 客户端 hosts 文件中缺少 Data Box Blob 服务端点（*.blob.*.microsoftdatabox.com）的 IP 映射条目，导致 DNS 名称解析失败
solution: 在客户端 hosts 文件中添加 Data Box Blob 服务端点条目（Windows: C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts；Linux: /etc/hosts）；端点地址和 IP 从 Data Box Web GUI 的 'Access Storage account and upload data' 页面获取
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to create a container on the Data Box using Azure Storage Explorer

symptom: 使用 Azure Storage Explorer 连接 Data Box 时报错：'The value for one of the HTTP headers is not in the correct format' 或 'Unable to retrieve child resources'
rootCause: Azure Storage Explorer 未配置为 Target Azure Stack 模式，导致发送的 HTTP 请求头格式与 Data Box（兼容 Azure Stack）不匹配
solution: 在 Azure Storage Explorer 中选择 Edit > Target Azure Stack，然后重启 Storage Explorer
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to create a container on the Data Box using Azure Storage Explorer

symptom: 在 Data Box 设备上通过 Windows Explorer 删除或重命名文件夹时报错（无效字符、需要管理员权限、无法删除根目录下的文件夹）
rootCause: 文件夹名称包含无效字符，或文件夹以不正确的方式复制导致 Windows Explorer 无法处理；用户可能缺少必要的管理员权限
solution: 使用 robocopy 镜像空目录删除目标文件夹：先 mkdir c:\temp\empty 创建空目录，再执行 robocopy.exe c:\temp\empty <target_parent_dir> <folder_to_delete> /purge /B；若问题持续收集 Process Monitor 日志并创建 IcM 升级 DataPath 工程团队
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to delete files or folders from Data Box

symptom: Unable to mount NFS share on Azure Data Box from Linux host; cannot copy data via NFS
rootCause: NFS client IP not whitelisted in Data Box local web UI; NFS client not installed on Linux host; or files copied directly to root folder instead of subfolder
solution: 1) In Data Box local web UI > Connect and copy > NFS settings > add client IP. 2) Install supported NFS client for Linux distro. 3) Mount: sudo mount <IP>:/<share> <mountpoint>. For Mac: add -o sec=sys,resvport. 4) Always create subfolder under share before copying
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to mount NFS share of Azure Data Box

symptom: Unable to mount NFS share on Azure Data Box from Linux host; NFS mount command fails; cannot copy data to Data Box over NFS
rootCause: NFS client IP not whitelisted in Data Box NFS settings; NFS client package not installed on Linux host; incorrect mount command syntax (Mac clients missing sec=sys,resvport options)
solution: 1) Data Box local Web UI > Connect and copy > NFS settings > NFS client access: add Linux host IP. 2) Install NFS client on Linux host. 3) Mount: sudo mount <IP>:/<share> <mount_point>. 4) For Mac: sudo mount -t nfs -o sec=sys,resvport <IP>:/<share> <mount>. 5) Always create subfolder under share before copying (subfolder becomes container in Azure). Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databox/data-box-deploy-copy-data-via-nfs
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to mount NFS share of Azure Data Box

symptom: Data Copy Service fails with error 'Could not complete the copy job' when source storage is Dell Isilon OS and target is Azure File Storage on Data Box
rootCause: Dell Isilon OS does not support System Access Control Lists (SACLs). Data Copy Service attempts to copy both DACLs and SACLs to Azure Files; since Isilon does not support SACLs and there is no option to skip them, the operation fails
solution: 1) Change target storage from Azure File Storage to Block Blob in Data Copy Service. 2) Alternatively, mount SMB share on Data Box and use robocopy: net use \\[DataBoxIP]\[share] /u:[DataBoxIP]\[user], then robocopy [Source] [Target] * /e /r:3 /w:60 /is /nfl /ndl /np /MT:32 /fft /B /Log+:[LogFile]. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databox/data-box-deploy-copy-data
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy Service/Data Copy Service Failure with Dell Isilon OS as Storage Source

symptom: Need to erase data on Data Box Pod when copy job is stuck in paused state and 'prepare to ship' cannot be run from Web UI
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-erase-data-box-pod-cli.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy Service/How to Erase Data on Data Box Pod from CLI (PowerShell Support Session)

symptom: Data Box device received at Azure datacenter but data copy to storage account has not started; order status shows Received but no progress on data transfer
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: shipping delay (device not yet at DC), delay at Azure DC in moving job to next state, device hardware issue, multiple retries due to DC issue, customer changed/deleted storage account, unit not connected with network adapters at DC, unit damaged in transit
solution: 1) Check ASC: verify AtAzureDC stage status shows succeeded. 2) Check Shipping Status in ASC for shipping details. 3) Check Data Transfer Status in ASC. 4) If issue persists, create support case under: Windows Azure > Azure Databox > Technical > Issues in Datacenter > DC operations > select topic based on exact issue
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/Data Box copy not yet started

symptom: Data Box device lost during return shipping; device shipped by customer has not been received at Azure datacenter
rootCause: Device was lost by shipping carrier (FedEx/UPS/DHL) during return transit to Azure datacenter
solution: 1) Check tracking in ASC: Resource Explorer > Shipping Status > reverse shipping type for return tracking URL. 2) Check courier details (FedEx/UPS/DHL) using tracking URL. 3) If courier shows no progress/movement, create ICM with DBX Operations Team for assistance. Ref: How to create an IcM for Data Box wiki page
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/Data Box device lost

symptom: Data copied to Data Box and uploaded to Azure Storage Account without errors, but data size shown on Azure Portal (Container Properties > Calculate Size) differs from what was copied to the device
rootCause: The Calculate Size feature on Azure Portal Storage Account Container properties is unreliable and may not reflect exact data size values
solution: 1) Verify no copy errors from Data Box upload. 2) Use more reliable methods to check actual container size: a) Azure Support Center: Storage Account > Capacity > Account Capacity Summary, b) Azure Storage Explorer: Containers > select container > Folder Statistics (recommended for customers), c) PowerShell: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/scripts/storage-blobs-container-calculate-size-powershell
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/Data copied without errors from Databox but size seems different in Storage Account

rootCause: The file optimization feature enabled by default in Data Copy Service WebUI packs files smaller than 1 MB during ingestion to speed up data copy for small files, creating ADB_PACK_* packed files
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy Service/Files copied in an unknown format (ADB_PACK_*) when using the built-in Data Copy Service
solution: Do not delete or modify ADB_PACK_* files; they are temporary and will be automatically unpacked and restored to original format when data is uploaded from Data Box to Azure Storage
symptom: Files copied via Data Box Data Copy Service appear as unknown files with ADB_PACK_* format instead of original file names

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/Data copy from Data Box to correct storage account but wrong storage type
solution: No server-side fix. Use AzCopy to move data between blob and file storage: 1) Identify where data was ingested (Storage Explorer or BOM files). 2) Generate SAS token. 3) Run: azcopy copy blob-url file-url --recursive. 4) Verify then delete source. No refund for user error.
symptom: Customer copies data to wrong folder on Data Box (e.g., block blob folder instead of Azure Files or vice versa), data ingested into wrong storage type
rootCause: User error: data copied to incorrect storage type folder on Data Box device (e.g., block blob share instead of file share)

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/File uploaded in lease expired state were unavailable on file share
solution: 1) Create ICM with DBX Operations for Hold for Investigation. 2) Verify no other app has lease. 3) Check if using Data Box Gateway or File Share syncing. 4) Complete file share sync, unmount share, disconnect Gateway, remap drives, reconnect. 5) Verify files accessible.
symptom: Data Box upload completes but some files fail with Error Code 412: There is currently a lease on the blob and no lease ID was specified. Blobs in Lease Expired state
rootCause: Another application (Data Box Gateway, File Share Syncing) acquired lease on target blobs, or data churn during upload. Duplicate data to same container from prior DBX disk upload.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/Size on Data Box differs from size in Azure Storage Account
solution: Check source NAS for physical vs logical size. Explain: logical size (Azure) is smaller because Data Box allocates by physical size, Azure stores logical. File count match confirms successful copy.
symptom: Data copied to Data Box and uploaded to Azure, file count matches but total size on NAS source differs significantly from Azure Storage size
rootCause: NAS devices (EMC Epsilon/Isilon) report physical size (with data protection) vs logical size (actual content). Data Box uses physical size, Azure stores logical size.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Device Return - Logistics/Customer dropped off device as USPS instead of UPS
solution: 1) Create ICM with Operations Team. 2) Email adbops@microsoft.com with order details. 3) If device lost, share pricing: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/databox/
symptom: Customer shipped Data Box via USPS instead of UPS, tracking unavailable, device location unknown
rootCause: Customer used wrong carrier (USPS) instead of designated carrier (UPS)

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Device Return - Logistics/Device Lost by UPS
solution: 1) Create ICM with Operations Team. 2) Email adbops@microsoft.com with order/tracking details. 3) If investigation too long, customer re-creates job (no charge for incomplete job).
symptom: Data Box returned via UPS/DHL but delivery delayed beyond expected timeframe, device not received at datacenter
rootCause: Device delayed or lost by carrier (UPS/DHL) during return transit

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Device Return - Logistics/Device Pickup Issues
solution: 1) Create ICM with Operations Team. 2) Email adbops@microsoft.com to arrange collection. 3) Ops team arranges pickup without fee. 4) Follow up on collection date.
symptom: In Europe, UPS/DHL requests pickup fee from customer for Data Box return, even though return service is included
rootCause: Carrier incorrectly charging collection fee; device return service is included in Data Box pricing

symptom: Data Box export order fails or no data exported due to incorrect XML file formatting; XML tags case-sensitive mismatch, opening/closing tags not matching, or invalid blob/file prefixes
rootCause: XML file used for Data Box export order has formatting errors: tags are case-sensitive and must match exactly; incorrect formatting leads to data export failure with no data exported
solution: Follow the sample XML file table specifications from Microsoft documentation; ensure XML tags are case-sensitive match, opening and closing tags match, and blob/file prefixes are valid. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databox/data-box-deploy-export-ordered
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Export Jobs/Assistance with creation of XML file

symptom: Data Box export order error when attempting to export managed disks; log file not loading, requested VHD file not copied to device
rootCause: Exporting managed disks via Data Box is not a supported use case
solution: Inform customer that managed disk export via Data Box is not supported; customer needs to cancel the order and use an alternative method (e.g., export disk as VHD to storage account first, then use Data Box)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Export Jobs/Data Box Export order error for managed disk

symptom: Data Box export job from Azure halted or fails; files not copied during export; etag mismatch in copy logs between beforeExportEtag and afterExportEtag
rootCause: Data churn during export operation: files (e.g., VHDs) were modified by running VMs while the export was in progress, causing etag changes that halt the copy process
solution: Create ICM with Data Box Operations to ship device as-is so customer can check exported data; for failed files, customer must create new export order and ensure no VM writes to VHDs during export operation; check copylog container in storage account for etag mismatch details
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Export Jobs/Data churn impacting export job

symptom: Data Box device unable to power up on arrival; no activity lights; system fault indicator LED is red indicating fan failure, high CPU/motherboard temperature, or DIMM ECC error
rootCause: Hardware failure: possible fan failure, CPU temperature too high, motherboard temperature too high, or DIMM ECC error; device may be dead on arrival (DOA)
solution: Verify proper power connection and try different outlet; check front panel LED indicators (bottom LED = system fault indicator); if red: check fan, move device to location with greater airflow; if unresolved, create ICM with Data Box Ops team to arrange device return/replacement
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Hardware/Data Box dead on arrival (DOA)

symptom: Data Box power supply unit (PSU) is faulty; device does not power on or has intermittent power issues
rootCause: No field replaceable units in Data Box; any hardware issue including PSU failure requires device return
solution: Customer must return the device; there is no field replaceable unit. Create ICM with Data Box Ops team to arrange return and replacement device
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Hardware/Hardware FAQ

symptom: Data Box Pod delivered without power cable; no power cord included in shipment
rootCause: Power cables were not included during shipping/packaging of the Data Box device
solution: Create ICM with Data Box Ops team to either ship the missing power cables to the customer, or customer can use any standard desktop computer power cord as a temporary solution
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Hardware/No power cord shipped with Data Box

symptom: Unable to insert, remove, or manipulate SFP+ cable or transceiver module on Data Box device; SFP+ module stuck in DATA1/DATA2 port
rootCause: Physical handling issue with SFP+ modules on Mellanox ConnectX-3 Pro EN Dual-Port 10GBASE-T adapter; customer may be using incompatible cables or handling incorrectly
solution: Customer should engage their network team or cable/transceiver vendor for physical handling assistance; recommend using supported Mellanox cables/switches tested in-house; if SFP is stuck and cannot be removed, create ICM with Data Box Ops team to arrange device return
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Hardware/Unable to insert or remove SFP cable on Data Box

solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-connecting-support-session-data-box.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Management and How-To/Connecting to a Support Session on a Data Box Device

symptom: Data Box Local UI shows OOPs Something went wrong with error code #60XZY (X=3 hardware unhealthy); virtual disk failed or all network adapters failed
rootCause: Hardware component failure on Data Box device - virtual disk in failed state or all network adapters failed
solution: Collect support package from limited OOBE UI; contact management and platform team; device may need replacement
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Management and How-To/Local UI Error Codes

symptom: Data Box Local UI shows OOPs Something went wrong with error code #60XYZ (Y=2 software unhealthy); DV filter service not running or exception unlocking disks
rootCause: Software health failure - DV filter service not running, exception trying to unlock disks, or pod startup tasks failed
solution: Collect support package; contact management and platform team; try rebooting the appliance
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Management and How-To/Local UI Error Codes

symptom: Data Box Local UI shows OOPs Something went wrong with error code #55XYZ; management cluster resource not running or responding
rootCause: Management cluster resource on Data Box device is not running or not responding
solution: Reboot the appliance; if persists collect support package and contact management and platform team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Management and How-To/Local UI Error Codes

symptom: Data Box Local UI shows OOPs Something went wrong with error code #70111; unexpected error for a UI action
rootCause: Data Box returned unexpected error for a UI action; transient or unknown software issue
solution: Reboot the appliance and retry the operation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Management and How-To/Local UI Error Codes

symptom: Data Box Download file list (BOM) button not shown in Local UI or greyed out after Prepare to Ship
rootCause: UI rendering issue or Prepare to Ship did not complete properly
solution: Refresh page; log off and log in again; retry Prepare to Ship by unlocking device first
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Management and How-To/Unable to download BOM Files

symptom: Data Box Download file list (BOM) button visible but clicking results in error; BOM file not present
rootCause: BOM file not generated during Prepare to Ship process due to incomplete or failed preparation
solution: Retry Prepare to Ship: unlock device first then perform Prepare to Ship; if persists collect support package
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Management and How-To/Unable to download BOM Files

symptom: Need to change shipping address for Data Box order after it has been placed; edit shipping address option unavailable after device dispatched
rootCause: Shipping address edit is only available in Azure portal before the device is dispatched; once dispatched the self-service option is no longer available
solution: Go to Order details > Edit shipping address to edit and validate the new address before dispatch; if device already dispatched, contact Data Box Operations team for further assistance
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Ordering - Logistics/Change Shipping Address

symptom: Data Box order showing incorrect region (e.g., West US) instead of customer-selected region (e.g., East US); order created in unexpected deployment region
rootCause: Data Box devices are allocated at nearest data center with in-stock availability; in the US, order deployment region always shows WestUS because Data Box product team is based in Western US; this only affects the Data Box resource/order metadata, not the target storage account
solution: This is expected behavior; verify target storage account regions in ASC summary tab; order deployment region for US orders is always WestUS regardless of storage account location; data will be uploaded to the storage account region specified in the order
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Ordering - Logistics/Data Box order showing incorrect region

symptom: Data Box ordering and logistics FAQs: storage account change, country availability, delivery timeline, order processing duration
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-data-box-ordering-faqs.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Ordering - Logistics/Ordering FAQs

symptom: Customer requests Data Box to be shipped to a different address than original order; need to redirect delivery destination after order placement
rootCause: Address change after order placement requires Data Box Operations team approval and coordination; cannot be fully self-serviced through portal
solution: Create ICM to Data Box Ops team requesting address change with order details; Ops team will seek stakeholder approvals and process the request; for return shipment from different address, request new shipping label from Ops team since original label has different return address
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Ordering - Logistics/Shipping Data Box to different address

symptom: Data Box device not delivered on expected date by courier service (UPS/DHL); delivery delayed beyond expected timeframe; customer reports missed or late delivery
rootCause: Regional carrier (UPS/DHL) delivery delay or missed delivery; tracking shows dispatched but device not received by customer
solution: Raise ICM (Sev3) to Data Box Ops team to open investigation case with carrier; also email Operations team using template: Subject 'Azure Data collection | <order-name>' with customer info, ICM ID, order details, serial number, and reverse tracking number from ASC
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Ordering - Logistics/The Data Box was not delivered on the expected date by courier service

symptom: Customer unable to download Data Box shipping label from portal or needs new shipping label for return; customer and device in different locations
rootCause: Shipping label unavailable in portal or customer needs label for different return location
solution: Verify Prepare to Ship completed without errors; for EU orders check e-ink display for label; if unavailable, create ICM for Data Box Ops Team and email adbops@microsoft.com with order details and ICM#
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/Customer needs new shipping label

symptom: Data Box Prepare to Ship stuck; hcsdpservice.Primary logs show SE_METADATA_NOT_FOUND error; process does not complete
rootCause: Files copied to Data Box were in use by another application or process during copy, causing metadata corruption (SE_METADATA_NOT_FOUND)
solution: Collect support package; search hcsdpservice.Primary logs for SE_METADATA_NOT_FOUND; if error points to a file, delete and recopy it; if error points to a folder, delete entire folder and recopy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/Prepare to Ship Stuck with SE_METADATA_NOT_FOUND error

symptom: Unable to run Prepare to Ship on Data Box due to 502 error on Local Web UI; cannot access local UI to manage device
rootCause: Known issue where Data Box Local Web UI returns 502 error, preventing access to the management interface and Prepare to Ship process
solution: If data copy is complete, shutdown device using power button and return to Azure DC; create ICM with DBX Pod Management team to confirm; request return shipping labels from Ops team; DO NOT power on again after shutdown as shares will be locked
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/Unable to run prepare to ship due to 502 error on Local Web UI of Data Box

symptom: Data Box Prepare to Ship fails with ERROR_CONTAINER_OR_SHARE_CAPACITY_EXCEEDED when uploading more than 5TB to Azure File Share
rootCause: Azure storage account file share capacity limit of 5TB exceeded; large file shares not enabled on the storage account
solution: Enable large file shares on storage account (Configuration > Large file shares > Enabled); enable Disregard LFS errors in local Web UI (Connect and Copy > Settings); re-run Prepare to Ship; if shipping label not shown, download from Azure portal or create ICM with Ops
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/Uploading shares that are larger than 5 TB to Data Box Share

symptom: Customer needs to copy managed disk to Azure storage account for export via Import-Export or Data Box
solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-copying-managed-disk-to-storage-account.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Import-Export Service/Copying A Managed Disk to an Azure Storage Account

symptom: Customer receives incorrect files in Azure Import-Export export job; unwanted snapshots fill the drives instead of intended data
rootCause: Customer selected Export all option instead of specific files/containers; all snapshots in storage account (including Azure Site Recovery snapshots) exported until drives full
solution: Use Selected containers and blobs or Export from blob list file (XML) option instead of Export all; if using Azure Site Recovery, temporarily stop or modify snapshot retention settings before export
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Import-Export Service/Incorrect Files Sent to Customer for Export Job

symptom: Unable to create Azure Import-Export import job in portal; error related to journal file (.jrn) from waimport.exe tool
rootCause: Journal file is either incomplete (missing XML output at end) or exceeds the 2MB limit (waimport.exe v1 only)
solution: Check journal file for complete XML at end; if incomplete: v1 - regenerate with /skipwrite parameter; v2 - recopy data to drives; if exceeds 2MB limit: use the _DriveInfo_<SerialID>.xml file instead to create the import job
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Import-Export Service/Unable to Create Import Job due to Journal File

symptom: Need reference for active root cause classifications in Dynamics 365 for Azure Storage Devices
solution: See SharePoint Root Cause Tree and Instructions links for A&I Support Planning and Onboarding
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Root Cause Tree/Classifications Active Root Cause Classification - Dynamics 365

symptom: Need access to Config Hub for Azure Storage Devices root cause classification
rootCause: User not a member of Confighub-Product-Author role
solution: Go to CoreIdentity (https://coreidentity.microsoft.com) and request Confighub-Product-Author membership
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Root Cause Tree/Request Access to Config Hub

symptom: Unable to generate activation key for Azure Stack Edge (ASE); 403 error: Resource was disallowed by policy
rootCause: Azure Policy blocking creation of public endpoints, or user account lacks owner/contributor rights on the ASE resource
solution: Verify account has owner or contributor rights; check for Azure Policy blocking endpoint creation; remove or adjust the restrictive policy to allow activation key generation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Activation Key/403 Error when Generating Activation Key
Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/GPU - Compute/Disable Multi-Process Service MPS on Azure Stack Edge
solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-ase-disable-mps.md
symptom: Need to disable Multi-Process Service (MPS) on Azure Stack Edge GPU VM

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Hardware/Data Disk and PSU Replacement
solution: Open an ICM with Data Box Edge Platform team for any data disk and PSU replacements; these are FRUs handled by the platform team
rootCause: Data disks and PSUs are Field Replaceable Units (FRUs) that may fail and require replacement
symptom: Need to replace data disk or power supply unit (PSU) on Azure Stack Edge device; hardware component failure
Customer wants to monitor Azure Stack Edge hardware temperature; temperature not available in Azure portal metrics
rootCause: Temperature monitoring is not included in Azure Stack Edge available metrics; only accessible via BMC (iDRAC) connection
solution: Configure BMC interface via PowerShell with IP/Gateway/Subnet/Password; connect BMC port to host machine; access iDRAC portal at BMC IP with edgeuser or edgesupport credentials to view temperature dashboard
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Hardware/Monitor Hardware Temperature on Azure Stack Edge
