solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-ase-device-replacement.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Hardware/Replacing an Azure Stack Edge Device

symptom: Need to reseat or replace a data drive on Azure Stack Edge Pro device
solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-ase-reseat-replace-data-drive.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Hardware/Reseating or Replacing a Data Drive on Azure Stack Edge Pro

symptom: Customer running Azure Private 5G Core (AP5GC) or AKS on Azure Stack Edge; case may need rerouting to Azure For Operators (A4O) team
rootCause: AP5GC and AKS on ASE issues are owned by Azure For Operators team, not Azure Storage Devices team; AP5GC uses custom AKS deployment instead of native Kubernetes
solution: Ask customer if running 5G workloads on ASE; if yes, reroute case to Azure For Operators support team; SAP for AP5GC and AKS on ASE: Azure -> Azure Private 5G Core
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Identifying AP5GC Cases and Customers for Rerouting and Collaboration

symptom: Need to change memory or processor limits for Kubernetes worker node on Azure Stack Edge device
solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-ase-k8s-memory-processor-limits.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Kubernetes/Changing Memory Processor Limits for Kubernetes on Azure Stack Edge

solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-ase-k8s-admin-access.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Kubernetes/Granting Kubernetes Admin Access on Azure Stack Edge
symptom: Customer requires Kubernetes admin cluster access on Azure Stack Edge; default aseuser lacks admin access

rootCause: K8s VMs default to 4GB memory; custom VMs depend on image used; workload requires more memory than allocated
solution: Enter support session; Get-VM to find VM name; Stop-VM; Set-VMMemory -VMName <name> -StartupBytes <amount>; Start-VM. For K8s VMs: get approval from ASE GPU Tiger Team first; may need to restart K8s cluster group. Memory range: 15%-60% of device spec (max 65% with env variable override)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Kubernetes/Increase K8 VM memory
symptom: Azure Stack Edge Kubernetes or custom VM showing alerts/errors for low or insufficient memory; VM performance degraded

rootCause: Bug in ASE builds 2301/2303: K8s v1.24 no longer auto-generates service account tokens; refreshing K8s dashboard certs removes old dashboard+token and new dashboard is created without token (Bug 17941654)
solution: Enable support session; RDP to ASE; edit kubernetesdashboard.yaml at G:\HcsExternal\Kubernetes\KubeVM\LinuxScripts\kubernetes\manifests\ to add Secret manifest for kubernetes-dashboard service account token; run Set-HcsKubernetesClusterDSC; wait 5-10 min for reconciliation; verify with Get-HcsKubernetesDashboardToken; download new config from WebUI
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Kubernetes/Unable to access kubernetes dashboard
symptom: Unable to access Kubernetes dashboard on Azure Stack Edge; Get-HcsKubernetesDashboardToken returns empty; neither cert nor token auth works for dashboard login

solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-ase-bmc-connection.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/Connecting to an Azure Stack Edge Device via BMC
symptom: Need to connect to Azure Stack Edge device via BMC/iDRAC for hardware troubleshooting

solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-ase-elevated-support-session.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/Elevated Support Session
symptom: Need to enter elevated support session on Azure Stack Edge or Data Box Gateway device

solution: Collect support package from ASE device; BIOS version can be found at \cmdlets\platform\systeminfo.txt in the support package
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/Finding the BIOS version of an Azure Stack Edge device
rootCause: BIOS version information is not directly accessible via portal or CLI; it is only available in the support package
symptom: Need to find BIOS version of Azure Stack Edge device

solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-ase-performance-logs.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/Generating and Reading Performance Logs on Azure Stack Edge
symptom: Need to generate and read performance logs on Azure Stack Edge to diagnose high or unusual data transfer volume

solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-ase-move-subscription.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/Move Azure Stack Edge Resource Between Azure Subscriptions
symptom: Need to move Azure Stack Edge resource between Azure subscriptions; customer wants to transfer ASE device to different subscription

solution: Connect to BMC at 192.168.100.100 (configure host adapter with static IP 192.168.100.5/24); log in as EdgeUser; open Virtual Console; Ctrl-Alt-Del to unlock; enter 50-digit Bitlocker Recovery Key. If key lost: Azure portal > ASE resource > Settings > Security > Secrets to retrieve it from Key Vault
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/Recovering an Azure Stack Edge from the Bitlocker Recovery Screen (Local WebUI is Inaccessible)
rootCause: Device unable to boot past Bitlocker due to certain issues that occurred on the device; 50-digit Bitlocker Recovery Key must be entered to continue boot
symptom: Azure Stack Edge unable to boot to OS; device stuck at Bitlocker Recovery screen; Local WebUI inaccessible even though device appears powered on

solution: Enter support session; Stop-ClusterResource hcsmgmt; reg delete HKLM\Cluster\HCS\Datastore\WorkEngineInfo\<failed-job-guid> /f (find GUID in hcsmgmt.Primary logs); Start-ClusterResource hcsmgmt. If hcsedgeagentwriter error follows, repeat for that service. Update device to latest version to prevent recurrence
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/Unable to Access Local Web UI
rootCause: Bug 7297787 (fixed post update 1911): hcsmgmt service fails due to ArgumentNullException when trying to restart incomplete orchestrated job; work item not found causes TimeoutException
symptom: Unable to access Azure Stack Edge Local WebUI; Lost heartbeat alerts in Azure portal; hcsmgmt service in failed state

solution: Enter Support Session; run Get-HcsNetInterface to view current MTU values per port; run Set-HcsNetInterface -InterfaceAlias <Port#> -MTU <value> to change; may need NodeID; verify with Get-HcsNetInterface again
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Networking/Changing the Network Interface MTU Value
symptom: Need to change MTU value on Azure Stack Edge network interface/port for proper network communication
rootCause: Customer internal network requires specific MTU size; ASE default MTU may not match customer network configuration; most networks auto-negotiate but some require manual setting

solution: Issue #1: Push cable firmly and hold 10sec; try bending latch tab up on RJ45 connector; secure cable in working position with cable tie. Issue #2: No field mitigation possible; customer must return device and order replacement. Both issues corrected in General Release units
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Networking/Failure to Establish Reliable Link
symptom: Azure Stack Edge Pro 2 Port 1 or Port 2 (10/1GbE RJ45) fails to establish link, links intermittently, or only links when cable is manipulated; LEDs off or intermittent
rootCause: Two known issues on Private Preview units: 1) RJ45 connector design issue causing inconsistent electrical connection; 2) LAN Transformer component has defective solder joints from degraded plating finish on SMT pins

solution: Option 1: Delete vSwitch on old port via PowerShell, restart ASE, create new vNIC with new IP, remove old vNIC. Option 2: Connect and configure IP on old port, restart ASE. Option 3: Connect to ARM, Remove-AzNetworkInterface -Force for all vNICs, disable compute on port 2, re-enable compute on port 2
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Networking/Issues with updating IP Subnet on vNET
symptom: Unable to update IP subnet on virtual network (vNET) for Azure Stack Edge VM; VM still shows old IP; new NIC creation fails; VNET shows old subnet
rootCause: Another port was previously configured for compute and disconnected after reconfiguration; ASE device moved to new location and VMs reconfigured but old port 3 compute config remained

solution: Add /dcopy:x option to Robocopy command to skip copying alternate data streams; files should then copy successfully
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Storage Gateway/Copy Error 665
symptom: Copy to Azure Stack Edge via Robocopy fails with ERROR 665 (0x00000299) - The requested operation could not be completed due to a file system limitation
rootCause: Azure Stack Edge uses ReFS internally which limits alternate data streams to 128KiB; source files with larger alternate data streams (thumbnails, metadata for images) exceed this limit

solution: Check hcsmgmt.Primary logs for BlobImmutableDueToLegalHold error; remove legal hold from the storage container following Azure docs on immutability policies; once legal hold removed, missing data will be re-uploaded automatically
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Storage Gateway/Unable to write Data to Azure Stack Edge due to Legal Hold on Storage Account
symptom: Unable to write data to Azure Stack Edge; upload failed error; hcsmgmt logs show BlobImmutableDueToLegalHold error with status 409
rootCause: Legal hold set on storage container associated with the ASE device makes blobs immutable; prevents upload/modification of data

symptom: Cannot update IP Subnet for Virtual Network on Azure Stack Edge VM; subnet shows old IP config; cannot deploy new VM or add NIC with new subnet
rootCause: Another port was previously configured for compute and disconnected, leaving stale compute configuration
solution: Option 1: Delete vSwitch on old compute port via PowerShell, restart ASE, recreate vNIC. Option 2: Reconnect Port and configure IP, restart ASE. Option 3: Via ARM, remove all vNICs (Get-AzNetworkInterface | Remove-AzNetworkInterface -Force), disable/re-enable compute on port
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Networking/Issues with updating IP Subnet on vNET

symptom: Robocopy to Azure Stack Edge fails with ERROR 665 (0x00000299): requested operation could not be completed due to file system limitation
rootCause: Azure Stack Edge uses ReFS internally with 128kiB limit on alternate data streams; source files with large thumbnail/metadata streams exceed this limit
solution: Add /dcopy:x flag to Robocopy command to skip copying alternate data streams
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Storage Gateway/Copy Error 665

symptom: Unable to upload files to Azure Stack Edge share; 2998 error in ManifestsPerShare Errors.xml; hcsedgeagentwriter logs show WINDOWS ERROR (0x5) at DvFilterWrapper::OpenVectorHandle
rootCause: The datadata folder on ASE share root has ReadOnly attribute set, blocking the writer process
solution: Create empty non-readonly folder on host; robocopy it to share root with /nodcopy option to reset directory attributes; wait ~15 mins for writer to retry
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Storage Gateway/Unable to upload files on ASE due to readonly being set on the share's datadata folder

rootCause: File system corruption from disk resize operation on ASE VM
solution: Connect to VM console; Option 1: create repair VM, attach disk, attempt fix; Option 2: rebuild VM from scratch
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Virtual Machines/VM Fails to Boot Up after Resize
symptom: VM on Azure Stack Edge fails to boot after disk resize; OS error 0xC0000098 required file missing

rootCause: Windows Server trial version expiration: wlms.exe initiates automated shutdown after 180 days
solution: Check EventLog for wlms.exe shutdown pattern; extend trial period (up to 6 times, ~3 years); or activate with full license
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Virtual Machines/Windows Server VM Stopping on its Own
symptom: Windows Server VM on Azure Stack Edge stops unexpectedly after ~180 days; VM shows stopped in portal; repeats multiple times per day
Cannot configure web proxy with NTLM authentication from Azure Stack Edge Local WebUI; authentication option not available after device reset
solution: Configure proxy via console support session using: Set-HcsWebProxy -AuthType NTLM -ConnectionURI <> -UserName <>
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Web Proxy/Cannot Configure Proxy with NTLM Authetnication from Local WebUI
rootCause: NTLM proxy configuration was removed from Local UI; Local UI does not support NTLM Authentication proxy configuration

symptom: Unable to apply web proxy on Azure Stack Edge from Local WebUI; errors: web proxy URL not valid or could not reach web proxy server; 403 Forbidden in hcsmgmt logs
solution: Test proxy with FQDN and port, then IP and port; run diagnostics and collect support package; test connectivity from Support Session (Ping, Tracert, Test-NetConnection); check hcsmgmt_primary logs for 403 errors; ensure all required URL patterns whitelisted per Azure docs especially https://www.msftconnecttest.com/connecttest.txt
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Web Proxy/Unable to Apply Web Proxy
rootCause: Required URL patterns not whitelisted on customer proxy; specifically msftconnecttest.com/connecttest.txt URL needed for ASE web proxy validation test; proxy may require authentication or block ASE endpoints

symptom: Case misrouted to Azure Storage Devices queue; common misroutes include PST migration, on-premises data gateway, Azure VM, AKS, Azure Stack Hub/HCI cases
solution: PST migration via network upload: reroute to Security > Purview > Import Service. On-premises data gateway: reroute to Logic App/Power Apps/Power Automate. Azure VMs: reroute to VM Running on Windows/Linux. AKS: reroute to Kubernetes Service. Azure Stack Hub: reroute to Azure Stack Hub. Azure Local (HCI): reroute to Azure Local. AP5GC/AKS on ASE: reroute to Azure Private 5G Core
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Storage Devices Wiki/Common Misroutes
rootCause: Keywords like gateway, edge, Azure Stack, import-export cause routing confusion with other Azure products

symptom: Need to set up and use Az.StackEdge PowerShell module for Azure Stack Edge automation
solution: Install Azure Az PowerShell module; Connect-AzAccount; verify subscription with Get-AzContext (Set-AzContext if needed); Install-Module -Name Az.StackEdge; then use Az.StackEdge cmdlets
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/Using Az.StackEdge CMDlets

symptom: Need to collect VM guest logs on Azure Stack Edge for issue diagnosis
solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-ase-collect-vm-logs.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Virtual Machines/Collect VM Guest Logs

symptom: Need to manually install GPU drivers on RHEL VM running on Azure Stack Edge; automatic GPU driver installation fails
solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-ase-gpu-drivers-rhel.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Virtual Machines/Manually Installing GPU Drivers on RHEL VM

symptom: Azure Stack Edge Local WebUI stuck on Configuring the Device screen on first access after imaging
rootCause: Bug in ASE software builds predating 2012: ConfigState set to Uninitialized or Standalone instead of Clustered after imaging
solution: Access BMC Virtual Console; verify state with Get-HcsApplianceInfo and Get-HcsControllerSetupInformation; set ConfigState to Clustered via registry Set-ItemProperty; refresh WebUI
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/WebUI Stuck on 'Configuring the Device'

symptom: Azure Stack Edge Pro 2 Port 1 or Port 2 LEDs always off, port completely fails to establish link under any circumstances
rootCause: LAN Transformer component has defective solder joints from degraded plating finish on SMT pins (Private Preview units only)
solution: No field mitigation; customer must return appliance and order replacement unit
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Networking/Failure to Establish Reliable Link

rootCause: Cluster Virtual Disk (ClusterPerformanceHistory) resource is Offline; can occur when updating from various builds
solution: Enter Support Session; Start-ClusterResource for ClusterPerformanceHistory; confirm Online; retry upgrade from Azure Portal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Update Issues/Azure Stack Edge upgrade failing due to Cluster Virtual Disk (ClusterPerformanceHistory) resource down
symptom: Azure Stack Edge upgrade failing from Azure Portal with error code NO_PARAM; update process fails

rootCause: Portal UI retry count of 6 is insufficient for low bandwidth networks (~20-30 Mbps); download times out before completion
solution: Upload image via ARM Templates or PowerShell cmdlets instead of Portal; if ARM also fails contact TA or create IcM; PG can upload via robocopy from Support Session
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Virtual Machines/BlobDownloadFailed Error - Adding VHD Image times out after 40-50 minutes
symptom: Adding VHD image to Azure Stack Edge fails with BlobDownloadFailed error after 40-50 minutes; image upload times out on low bandwidth network

rootCause: IP assigned to VM already in use by Kubernetes host IPs which ASE cannot detect; other causes: bad image, unreachable gateway/DNS, cloud-init issues
solution: Check IP conflicts with K8s host IPs; try different IP; verify image preparation; check gateway/DNS; for Linux verify cloud-init and waagent.conf
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Virtual Machines/ProvisioningTimeOut Error when deploying VM on Azure Stack Edge
symptom: VM deployment on Azure Stack Edge fails at last step with ProvisioningTimeOut error

System error 1909 when connecting to Data Box SMB share: 'The referenced account is currently locked out and may not be logged on to'. Account lockout after repeated failed authentication attempts via net use command.
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: (1) Using IP address with username format incorrectly, (2) Using device credentials instead of SMB access credentials, (3) Password contains special characters not properly escaped, (4) Password incorrectly copied or manually typed with errors.
solution: 1) Wait 30 minutes for account auto-unlock. 2) Use SMB access credentials (not device credentials) - verify in Azure Portal > Data Box > Device Details or local Web UI > Connect and copy > Access settings SMB. 3) If password has special characters, wrap in double quotes: net use \\IP\share /u:user "password_with$pecial%chars". 4) Copy-paste password from portal instead of typing manually.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Locked out of Data Box SMB share - "The reference account is currently locked out and may not be logged into"

symptom: System error 1331 when connecting to Data Box SMB share: 'This user can't sign in because this account is currently disabled'.
rootCause: The Data Box device has not been unlocked. Device needs to be unlocked via the local Web UI before SMB share access is possible.
solution: Have the customer unlock the device from the local Web UI (Connect and copy page), then retry accessing the SMB share.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Locked out of Data Box SMB share - "The reference account is currently locked out and may not be logged into"

symptom: SMB Session Authentication Failure on Data Box due to LmCompatibilityLevel mismatch. Account gets repeatedly locked after all other credential troubleshooting steps are exhausted.
rootCause: NTLM security settings mismatch between the client machine and the Data Box device. LAN Manager authentication level is not configured for NTLMv2, causing SMB authentication to fail.
solution: 1) Collect a Data Box support package for analysis. 2) Review Smbserver.Security event logs under ETW folder for SMB Session Authentication Failure and LmCompatibilityLevel mismatches. 3) On client machine: run secpol.msc > Local Policies > Security Options > Network Security: LAN Manager authentication level > Change to 'NTLMv2 response only/refuse LM and NTLM'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Locked out of Data Box SMB share - "The reference account is currently locked out and may not be logged into"

symptom: Data Copy Service fails with 'Could not complete the copy job. Check if shares are accessible from your device and retry the operation' when copying from Dell Isilon OS source to Azure File Storage on Data Box local Web UI.
rootCause: Dell Isilon OS does not support System Access Control Lists (SACLs). The Data Copy Service attempts to copy both DACLs and SACLs to import to Azure Files. Since SACLs are unsupported by Isilon OS and there is no option to skip SACLs in the Data Copy Service, the operation fails.
solution: Option 1: In Data Copy Service, change target storage from Azure File Storage to Block Blob (if customer is flexible on target). Option 2: Mount SMB share manually using 'net use \\[DeviceIP]\[share] /u:[DeviceIP]\[user]' and copy data using robocopy: 'robocopy [Source] [Target] * /e /r:3 /w:60 /is /nfl /ndl /np /MT:32 /fft /B /Log+:[LogFile]'. Refer to: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databox/data-box-deploy-copy-data
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy Service/Data Copy Service Failure with Dell Isilon OS as Storage Source

symptom: Unable to mount NFS share on Azure Data Box. Customer cannot copy data via NFS because the share mount fails.
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-Unable-to-mount-NFS-share-of-Azure-Data-Box.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Connect & Copy/Unable to mount NFS share of Azure Data Box

symptom: Files copied via Data Box copy service appear as unknown files with ADB_PACK_* format on the device
rootCause: The 'file optimization' feature in the Data Box WebUI is enabled by default, packing files smaller than 1 MB during ingestion to speed up the data copy process for small files
solution: Do not delete or modify ADB_PACK_* files. They are temporary and will be automatically unpacked when the data is uploaded to Azure, restoring them to their original format. The file optimization option can be seen/disabled in the WebUI copy service settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy Service/Files copied in an unknown format (ADB_PACK_*) when using the built-in Data Copy Service

symptom: Data Box data copy has not started at Azure datacenter after device status shows Received
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-data-box-copy-not-yet-started.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/Data Box copy not yet started

symptom: Data Box device lost during return shipping, not received at Azure datacenter
rootCause: Device lost by carrier (UPS/FedEx/DHL) during return shipment
solution: 1) Check tracking via ASC: Resource Explorer > Shipping Status > Reverse shipping type for return tracking URL. 2) Verify courier details (FedEx/UPS/DHL). 3) If tracking shows no delivery progress, create ICM with DBX Operations Team for investigation. Reference: How to create an IcM for Data Box wiki page.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/Data Box device lost

symptom: Data copied from Data Box to Azure Storage Account without errors, but data size on Azure Portal appears smaller than what was copied to the device
rootCause: The Azure Portal 'Calculate Size' feature on Storage Account Container properties does not reliably reflect exact data size values
solution: Verify actual data size using: 1) Azure Support Center (Storage Account > Capacity > Account Capacity Summary), 2) Storage Explorer - preferred for customers (Storage Account > Containers > select Container > Folder Statistics), 3) PowerShell script for container size calculation (https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/scripts/storage-blobs-container-calculate-size-powershell)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/Data copied without errors from Databox but size seems different in Storage Account

symptom: Data copied to Data Box in wrong folder type (e.g., block blob folder instead of Azure Files or vice versa), resulting in data uploaded to wrong storage type
rootCause: Customer copied data to incorrect storage type folder on Data Box device (e.g., block blob folder instead of file share folder)
solution: Use AzCopy to move data between storage types after upload. Syntax: azcopy copy 'https://<account>.blob.core.windows.net/<container>/<SAS>' 'https://<account>.file.core.windows.net/<share>/<SAS>' --recursive. After verification, delete original data to avoid double storage charges. No refund available for user error; customer may need to order new Data Box if AzCopy is not feasible.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/Data copy from Data Box to correct storage account but wrong storage type

symptom: Data Box upload fails with Error Code 412 'There is currently a lease on the blob and no lease ID was specified in the request', files appear in Lease Expired state on file share
rootCause: Another application (e.g., Data Box Gateway, On-Prem File Share Syncing) had acquired lease on the target blobs, causing conflict when Data Box tried to upload duplicate data to the same container
solution: 1) Create ICM with DBX Operations for 'Hold for Investigation'. 2) Verify no other application holds lease on target container. 3) Have customer complete file share sync, unmount share, disconnect Data Box Gateway, remap drives and reconnect. 4) Verify files become accessible after lease conflict is resolved.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/File uploaded in lease expired state were unavailable on file share

symptom: Total data size on Data Box is significantly larger than what appears in Azure Storage Account after upload, despite file count being correct
rootCause: NAS devices (such as EMC Epsilon/Isilon) report physical size (including data protection overhead) while Data Box copies at physical size. Azure Storage stores and reports logical file size which is smaller.
solution: Compare file counts instead of total size to verify data integrity. Physical size on NAS includes data protection overhead and is larger; Azure reports logical size which is expected to be smaller. Check if source NAS reporting tool can show both physical and logical sizes to confirm the difference.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Data Copy to Azure/Size on Data Box differs from size in Azure Storage Account

symptom: Data Box device shipped via USPS instead of UPS by customer, tracking information unavailable, device location unknown
rootCause: Customer dropped off device at wrong carrier (USPS instead of UPS)
solution: 1) Create ICM with Operations Team. 2) Email adbops@microsoft.com with subject 'Azure Data collection | <order-name>' including customer info and ICM ID. 3) Lost device charges may apply per Azure Data Box pricing (https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/databox/).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Device Return - Logistics/Customer dropped off device as USPS instead of UPS

symptom: Data Box device lost by UPS/DHL during return shipping, delivery taking longer than expected
rootCause: Carrier (UPS/DHL) lost or significantly delayed the device return shipment
solution: 1) Create ICM with Operations Team (ref: How to create an IcM for Data Box wiki). 2) Email adbops@microsoft.com with subject 'Azure Data collection | <order-name>' including customer info, order details, serial number, reverse tracking number, and ICM ID. 3) If investigation takes too long, recommend customer recreate the order and redo the copy; Operations team can mark incomplete job as no-charge.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Device Return - Logistics/Device Lost by UPS

symptom: UPS or DHL requesting pickup fee from customer when returning Data Box device, commonly seen in Europe
rootCause: Carrier incorrectly charging customer for device pickup that should be included in the Data Box service
solution: 1) Create ICM with Operations Team (ref: How to create an IcM for Data Box wiki). 2) Email adbops@microsoft.com with subject 'Azure Data collection | <order-name>' requesting collection arrangement without charge. Include customer info, order details, serial number, reverse tracking number. 3) Follow up between Ops team and customer on collection date; optionally wait until data upload completes before closing case.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Device Return - Logistics/Device Pickup Issues

symptom: Data Box Export order fails for managed disk - file not copied to device, log file not loading
rootCause: Exporting managed disks via Data Box is not a supported use case
solution: Inform customer that managed disk export via Data Box is unsupported. Customer needs to cancel the order. Alternative: first copy managed disk to a storage account blob, then export via Data Box.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Export Jobs/Data Box Export order error for managed disk

symptom: Data Box export job halted due to data churn - files fail to copy
rootCause: File etag changed between before and after export operation started (e.g., VM still writing to VHD during export), causing copy to fail for those files
solution: 1) Create ICM for Data Box Operations to allow device to ship so customer can check what was exported. 2) For missing data, create new order ensuring no data churn during export. 3) Ensure no VM is writing to VHD during export operation. Check copylog in storage account container 'copylog' for afterExportEtag vs before etag mismatch.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Export Jobs/Data churn impacting export job

symptom: Data Box device will not power up / dead on arrival (DOA) - no activity lights
rootCause: Hardware failure: possible fan failure, high CPU/motherboard temperature, or DIMM ECC error. System fault indicator LED (bottom light under power button) shows red.
solution: 1) Verify proper power connection, try different outlet. 2) Check front panel LEDs - red system fault indicator means hardware issue. 3) Check fan operation, move device to location with better airflow. 4) Collect support package from limited OOBE UI. 5) Create ICM with Data Box management/platform team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Hardware/Data Box dead on arrival (DOA)

symptom: No power cord shipped along with the Data Box device
rootCause: Power cables were not included when Data Box Pod was shipped to customer
solution: Create ICM with Data Box ops team informing power cable was not included. Ops team can either ship cables to customer, or customer can use any standard desktop computer power cord.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Hardware/No power cord shipped with Data Box

symptom: Unable to insert, remove, or handle SFP+ cable/transceiver on Data Box DATA1/DATA2 ports - SFP gets stuck
rootCause: Physical cabling issue with SFP+ module on Mellanox ConnectX-3 Pro EN Dual-Port 10GBASE-T adapter. Customer-provided SFP+ cable may not be compatible or is physically stuck.
solution: 1) Recommend customer engage their network team or cable/transceiver vendor for physical handling assistance. 2) Use supported Mellanox cables/switches where possible. 3) If SFP is stuck and customer cannot remove it for device return, create ICM with Data Box Ops team. Reference: Mellanox ConnectX3 FW release notes for supported cables.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Hardware/Unable to insert or remove SFP cable on Data Box

symptom: Data Box Local UI shows 'Oops! Something went wrong' with error code #60XYZ, #55XYZ, or #70111
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-local-ui-error-codes.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Management and How-To/Local UI Error Codes

symptom: Unable to download BOM files from Data Box Local UI - 'Download file list' button not shown, greyed out, or clicking results in error
rootCause: BOM file not present on device, or UI state issue
solution: 1) Try refreshing the page. 2) Log off and log back in to UI. 3) Retry 'Prepare to Ship': unlock the device first, then perform 'Prepare to Ship' again. 4) If issue persists, collect support package and contact management/platform team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Management and How-To/Unable to download BOM Files

symptom: Data Box order shows incorrect region (e.g., West US instead of expected East US)
rootCause: Data Box devices are allocated at the nearest datacenter with stock availability. In the US, the Order Deployment Region always shows WestUS (where product team is based). This does not affect the target storage account location.
solution: Clarify to customer: Order Deployment Region is where the service instance runs, not where data uploads to. Data will be uploaded to the storage account region they selected. Verify target storage accounts and regions in ASC under the Data Box resource summary tab. If error differs from this, create ICM with DBX Operations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Ordering - Logistics/Data Box order showing incorrect region

symptom: Customer wants to change Data Box shipping address to a different location after order is placed
rootCause: Logistics change request - address change not self-service in portal
solution: 1) Confirm order status in ASC. 2) Create ICM with Data Box Ops team requesting address change with required information. 3) Ops team seeks approvals from stakeholders, then prepares shipment. 4) For return: request Ops team to provide a new shipping label since return address differs from original order.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Ordering - Logistics/Shipping Data Box to different address

symptom: Data Box device not delivered on the expected date by courier service (UPS/DHL) - delivery delayed
rootCause: Regional carrier (UPS/DHL) delivery delay
solution: 1) Create ICM with Data Box Ops team to open investigation with carrier. 2) Send email to Ops team using template: Subject 'Azure Data collection | <order-name>', include customer name, location, ICM Sev3 ID, order details (job name, serial #), reverse tracking #. 3) Customer can track order status in Azure portal under DataBox order > Overview.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Ordering - Logistics/The Data Box was not delivered on the expected date by courier service

symptom: Data Box Prepare to Ship returns 'Scan completed with errors'. Customer cannot complete prepare to ship and e-ink shipping label is not updated.
rootCause: Files or containers on the Data Box violate Azure naming rules, exceed size limits, or contain unsupported file types. Specific errors include container/share capacity exceeded, illegal blob/file name characters, and unsupported data formats.
solution: Have customer unlock device, go to Connect and Copy in WebUI, and download the 'Issue list' folder. Review the XML file for exact errors and resolution URLs. Each error category has specific resolution steps documented at https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databox/data-box-troubleshoot
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/'Prepare to Ship' scan completed with errors

symptom: Data Box Prepare to Ship fails with ERROR_CONTAINER_OR_SHARE_CAPACITY_EXCEEDED. Azure file share exceeds 5 TiB limit.
rootCause: Large File Shares (LFS) was not enabled on the storage account before ordering the Data Box. Azure file share default limit is 5 TiB.
solution: 1) Enable Large File Shares on storage account (Configuration tab). 2) Create Azure File shares and set quota to 100 TB. 3) If LFS was enabled after ordering and prepare-to-ship fails with this as the only error, create ICM with Data Box Ops team to run ACIS operation for fallback shipping label process.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/'Prepare to Ship' scan completed with errors

symptom: Data Box Prepare to Ship error log shows ERROR_BLOB_OR_FILE_NAME_CHARACTER_ILLEGAL. File names displayed as random characters in error report.
rootCause: Files on the Data Box have names containing characters that violate Azure blob/file naming rules. The error report shows base64-encoded representation of the file path/name.
solution: Copy the file name from the error report and decode it using a base64 decoder (e.g., https://www.base64decode.org/) to identify the actual file path. Then rename or remove the offending files and re-run prepare to ship.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/'Prepare to Ship' scan completed with errors

symptom: Data Box Prepare to Ship error shows StorageRequestFailed. Upload fails after data copy.
rootCause: Two possible causes: 1) File rename operation conflicts during copy; 2) Legal hold or immutable storage policy enabled on the destination container blocks uploads.
solution: Scenario 1: Follow KB https://internal.support.services.microsoft.com/en-US/help/4550171 for file rename issue. Scenario 2: Have customer disable legal hold / immutable settings on the target container in Azure portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/'Prepare to Ship' scan completed with errors

symptom: Data Box Prepare to Ship process is stuck at 86% or more for days or weeks. No errors displayed but process does not complete.
rootCause: The device is busy preparing the BOM (Bill of Materials) file due to a very large number of files copied to the device. The BOM generation takes an extended time proportional to file count.
solution: 1) Request support package from customer. 2) Check hcs_status for BOM register and bomdirspersecond to confirm preparation is still running. 3) Check hcsdvservice.Primary logs for warnings/errors. 4) If BOM is taking too long, customer can ship the device without BOM files — the data will still upload to Azure (excluding files listed in copy log errors). 5) If everything looks normal, open ICM with DataPath team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/'Prepare to ship' process is stuck on Data Box

symptom: Customer is unable to download the Data Box shipping label from the Azure portal, or the device and customer are in different locations requiring a new label.
rootCause: The shipping label may not be available for download in the portal, or the customer needs a label sent to a different address than where the device is located.
solution: 1) Verify prepare to ship completed without errors. 2) For EU orders with e-ink display, label should be downloadable from portal. 3) If unavailable, create ICM with Data Box Ops team and email adbops@microsoft.com with subject 'Azure Data collection | <order-name>' including customer location, ICM#, order details, and reverse tracking number.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/Customer needs new shipping label

symptom: Data Box Prepare to Ship is stuck for days with no errors shown in local WebUI. Support package hcsdpservice.Primary logs show SE_METADATA_NOT_FOUND error.
rootCause: SE_METADATA_NOT_FOUND is caused by files that were in use by another application or process at the time they were copied to the Data Box device, resulting in corrupted metadata.
solution: 1) Collect support package from Data Box. 2) Search hcsdpservice.Primary logs for 'SE_METADATA_NOT_FOUND'. 3) If error points to a specific file, delete and recopy that file. 4) If error points to a folder, delete the entire folder and recopy it. 5) Re-run prepare to ship.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/Prepare to Ship Stuck with SE_METADATA_NOT_FOUND error

symptom: Customer cannot access Data Box Local Web UI to run Prepare to Ship, receiving HTTP 502 error.
rootCause: Known issue with Data Box local WebUI that causes 502 Bad Gateway error, preventing access to manage the device.
solution: This is a known issue. If data copy is complete: 1) Shut down the device using the power button (do NOT power on again to copy more data as shares will be locked). 2) Create ICM with DBX Pod Management team to confirm next steps. 3) Request return shipping label from Ops team via ICM. WARNING: Do not shutdown and restart — shares will be locked and cannot be unlocked due to the UI issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/Unable to run prepare to ship due to 502 error on Local Web UI of Data Box

symptom: After running Prepare to Ship on Data Box, error log shows ERROR_CONTAINER_OR_SHARE_CAPACITY_EXCEEDED when uploading more than 5TB to an Azure File Share.
rootCause: Azure file share default capacity limit is 5 TiB. Large File Shares (LFS) feature must be explicitly enabled on the storage account to support up to 100 TiB per share.
solution: 1) Enable Large File Shares on the storage account (Configuration > Large file shares > Enabled). 2) Under File Shares, set quota to 100 TiB or sufficient size. 3) In Data Box local UI, navigate to Connect and Copy > Settings > enable 'Disregard LFS errors'. 4) Re-run Prepare to Ship. 5) If e-ink display doesn't update, download and print shipping label from Azure portal. 6) If label unavailable, create ICM with Data Box Ops team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Data Box/Prepare to Ship/Uploading shares that are larger than 5 TB to Data Box Share

symptom: Customer received incorrect files (including unwanted snapshots) as part of their Azure Import-Export Service export job.
rootCause: Customer selected 'Export all' option when creating the export job instead of specifying exact files/containers. All blobs including snapshots from solutions like Azure Site Recovery were exported until the drives were full.
solution: 1) Use 'Selected containers and blobs' or 'Export from blob list file (XML format)' option instead of 'Export all'. 2) If using Azure Site Recovery or other snapshot-generating solutions, temporarily stop snapshots or modify retention settings before export. 3) To check existing snapshots, navigate to storage account > container > VHD > Snapshots tab.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Import-Export Service/Incorrect Files Sent to Customer for Export Job

symptom: Unable to create Import job in Azure portal using journal file (.jrn) generated by WAImportExport.exe tool. Portal shows error when uploading journal file.
rootCause: The journal file is incomplete — missing the XML metadata block at the end of the file that contains DriveId, BitLockerKey, ManifestFile and ManifestHash information.
solution: Regenerate the journal file by running WAImportExport.exe again. For v1 tool: use the /skipwrite parameter to avoid re-copying data to drives (e.g., ./WAImportExport.exe PrepImport /j:<journal> /id:session#<n> /t:<drive> /bk:<key> /srcdir:<drive>:\ /dstdir:<container>/ /blobtype:<type> /skipwrite). For v2 tool: data must be re-copied to the drives.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Import-Export Service/Unable to Create Import Job due to Journal File

symptom: Unable to create Import job in Azure portal — journal file (.jrn) generated by WAImportExport.exe v1 exceeds the 2MB size limit.
rootCause: WAImportExport.exe v1 can generate journal files larger than 2MB when preparing many files, and the Azure portal has a 2MB upload limit for journal files.
solution: Use the alternative file generated by the tool: <JournalFileName>_DriveInfo_<DriveSerialID>.xml to create the Import job in the Azure portal instead of the oversized .jrn file.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Import-Export Service/Unable to Create Import Job due to Journal File

symptom: Unable to generate activation key for Azure Stack Edge — HTTP 403 error 'Resource was disallowed by policy'
rootCause: Azure Policy on the subscription blocks creation of public endpoints, preventing activation key generation
solution: Ensure the user account has Owner or Contributor rights on the ASE resource; review and remove/exempt the Azure Policy that blocks public endpoint creation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Activation Key/403 Error when Generating Activation Key

symptom: Azure Stack Edge throws subject name error when uploading self-signed certificates created with New-SelfSignedCertificate
rootCause: DeviceName and/or NodeSerialNumber parameters in New-SelfSignedCertificate command do not match the actual ASE device name and serial number
solution: Verify that DeviceName and NodeSerialNumber parameters used in New-SelfSignedCertificate exactly match the values configured on the ASE device; regenerate certificates with correct parameters
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Certificates/Subject Name Error with Self-Signed Certificates

symptom: Data disk or PSU hardware failure on Azure Stack Edge device requires replacement
rootCause: Hardware component (data disk or power supply) failure — these are Field Replaceable Units (FRUs)
solution: Open an IcM with Data Box Edge Platform team via https://portal.microsofticm.com/imp/v3/incidents/create for disk/PSU replacement
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Hardware/Data Disk and PSU Replacement

symptom: Customer wants to monitor hardware temperature on Azure Stack Edge but no temperature metric available in Azure monitoring
rootCause: Azure Stack Edge monitoring/metrics do not include hardware temperature data; temperature is only accessible via BMC/iDRAC
solution: Connect to BMC: configure host NIC with static IP 192.168.100.5/24, connect to BMC port, browse to https://192.168.100.100, login with edgeuser/edgesupport and the configured BMC password. The iDRAC dashboard shows device temperature.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Hardware/Monitor Hardware Temperature on Azure Stack Edge

symptom: AP5GC or AKS-related issue on Azure Stack Edge needs routing to correct support team
solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-identifying-ap5gc-cases-rerouting.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Identifying AP5GC Cases and Customers for Rerouting and Collaboration

symptom: Low memory alerts or errors on Kubernetes VMs deployed on Azure Stack Edge device
rootCause: Default K8s VM memory limit is 4GB, insufficient for heavier workloads; custom VM memory depends on the image used
solution: Enter support session on ASE -> Get-VM to find VM name -> Stop-VM -> Set-VMMemory -VMName <name> -StartupBytes <new-value> -> Start-VM. For K8s VMs: get PG approval from ASE GPU Tiger Team first, then restart K8s cluster group with Get-ClusterGroup/Start-ClusterGroup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Kubernetes/Increase K8 VM memory

symptom: Unable to access Kubernetes dashboard on Azure Stack Edge after refreshing K8s certificates (builds 2301/2303), Get-HcsKubernetesDashboardToken returns empty
rootCause: Bug 17941654 in ASE builds 2301/2303: after v.124 upgrade, service account tokens are not auto-generated; certificate refresh removes old dashboard and token, new dashboard has no token for authentication
solution: Enable support session -> RDP to ASE -> edit kubernetesdashboard.yaml -> append Secret manifest (type: kubernetes.io/service-account-token, name: kubernetes-dashboard-token, namespace: kubernetes-dashboard) -> run Set-HcsKubernetesClusterDSC -> wait 5-10 min -> verify with Get-HcsKubernetesDashboardToken -> re-download config file from WebUI
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Kubernetes/Unable to access kubernetes dashboard

symptom: Need to determine the BIOS version of an Azure Stack Edge device
solution: Collect a support package from the ASE device, then find the BIOS version in \\cmdlets\\platform\\systeminfo.txt within the package
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/Finding the BIOS version of an Azure Stack Edge device

symptom: Azure Stack Edge device stuck at Bitlocker Recovery Screen, Local WebUI inaccessible despite device being powered on
rootCause: Device unable to boot to OS due to an issue that triggered Bitlocker recovery; requires 50-digit recovery key to proceed past the Bitlocker screen
solution: Connect to BMC (host static IP 192.168.100.5/24, BMC at https://192.168.100.100, login EdgeUser). Open Virtual Console -> Ctrl-Alt-Del -> enter Bitlocker Recovery Key. If key is lost: Azure Portal -> ASE resource -> Settings -> Security -> Secrets to retrieve the key from Key Vault.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/Recovering an Azure Stack Edge from the Bitlocker Recovery Screen (Local WebUI is Inaccessible)

symptom: Unable to access Local WebUI on Azure Stack Edge, 'Lost heartbeat from your device' alerts in Azure portal
rootCause: Bug 7297787 (fixed post update 1911) causes hcsmgmt service to enter failed state due to a failed orchestrator job stuck in registry, bringing down Local WebUI and heartbeat
solution: Enter support session -> Stop-ClusterResource hcsmgmt -> reg delete HKLM\\Cluster\\HCS\\Datastore\\WorkEngineInfo\\<failed-job-guid> /f -> Start-ClusterResource hcsmgmt. If hcsedgeagentwriter also fails, repeat stop/delete/start for that resource. Update device to latest version to prevent recurrence.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/Unable to Access Local Web UI

symptom: Azure Stack Edge WebUI stuck on 'Configuring the Device' screen after imaging; device fails to reach local web UI on first access (software version predating 2012 build)
rootCause: Bug in previous ASE software builds causes the device configuration state to remain 'Uninitialized' or 'Standalone' instead of 'Clustered' after imaging
solution: Access BMC console → Virtual Console → verify Get-HcsApplianceInfo shows SystemState 'Initialized' → run Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\HcsSetupService\Parameters' -Name 'ConfigState' -Value 'Clustered' → refresh WebUI page
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Manage Device/WebUI Stuck on 'Configuring the Device'

symptom: Azure Stack Edge Pro 2 Port 1 or Port 2 (10/1GbE RJ45) fails to establish link, link is intermittent, or only works when cable is manipulated
rootCause: RJ45 connectors for Port 1/2 have a design issue causing inconsistent electrical connection with inserted RJ45 patch cable (Issue #1, Private Preview units only)
solution: Push RJ45 cable firmly for ≥10 seconds; bend the plastic latch tab up slightly on the RJ45 cable connector and reinsert; if link establishes, secure cable in position with cable tie or weight. Corrective action: connector redesigned for General Release units
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Networking/Failure to Establish Reliable Link

symptom: Azure Stack Edge Pro 2 Port 1 or Port 2 completely fails to establish link under any circumstances; LEDs always off regardless of cable manipulation
rootCause: LAN Transformer component in Ethernet signal path has defective solder joints caused by degraded plating finish on SMT pins (Issue #2, Private Preview units only)
solution: No field mitigation possible for Issue #2. Customer must return the appliance and order a replacement. Corrective action: improved procurement control and enhanced manufacturing process controls for General Release
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Networking/Failure to Establish Reliable Link

symptom: Cannot update IP subnet for Virtual Network (vNET) on Azure Stack Edge VM; new VM deployment fails on NIC creation; adding new NIC only shows old IP subnet
rootCause: A previously configured compute port (e.g. Port 3) was disconnected after device reconfiguration/relocation, leaving stale vSwitch configuration that conflicts with new network settings
solution: Option 1: Delete stale vSwitch via PowerShell → restart ASE → create new vNIC with correct IP → remove old vNIC. Option 2: Connect and configure IP on the previously used port → restart ASE. Option 3: Connect to ARM → Remove-AzNetworkInterface -Force all vNICs → disable compute on active port → re-enable compute
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Networking/Issues with updating IP Subnet on vNET

symptom: Copy to Azure Stack Edge via Robocopy fails with ERROR 665 (0x00000299) — 'The requested operation could not be completed due to a file system limitation'
rootCause: Azure Stack Edge uses ReFS internally which limits alternate data streams to 128 KiB. Source files with larger alternate data streams (e.g. thumbnails/metadata for images) exceed this limit
solution: Add /dcopy:x option to the Robocopy command to skip copying alternate data streams
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Storage Gateway/Copy Error 665

symptom: Unable to upload files on Azure Stack Edge with error 2998; hcsedgeagentwriter logs show 'WINDOWS ERROR (0x5) error at DvFilterWrapper::OpenVectorHandle'; share root folder has ReadOnly attribute set
rootCause: The share's datadata folder has ReadOnly attribute set, causing the edge agent writer to fail with access denied (0x5) when trying to open vector handles for upload
solution: Create a non-readonly folder (mkdir empty), robocopy it to the affected share root with /nodcopy option to clear ReadOnly attribute, then wait ~15 mins for writer to retry upload. Verify with (get-item w:\).attributes showing 'Directory' without 'ReadOnly'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Storage Gateway/Unable to upload files on ASE due to readonly being set on the share's datadata folder

symptom: Unable to write/upload data to Azure Stack Edge; Azure portal shows 'Upload Failed'; copy logs show error 2998; hcsmgmt logs show 'BlobImmutableDueToLegalHold' with status 409
rootCause: Legal hold(s) set on the Azure Storage container associated with the ASE device, making blobs immutable and blocking write operations
solution: Remove the legal hold(s) from the storage container following Microsoft Learn instructions for configuring immutability policies (version-scope). Once removed, missing data will be re-uploaded automatically
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Storage Gateway/Unable to write Data to Azure Stack Edge due to Legal Hold on Storage Account

symptom: Azure Stack Edge update/upgrade from Azure Portal fails with error code 'NO_PARAM'
rootCause: Cluster Virtual Disk (ClusterPerformanceHistory) resource is in Offline state, blocking the update process
solution: From a Support Session, bring the resource online: Get-ClusterResource -Name 'Cluster Virtual Disk (ClusterPerformanceHistory)' | Start-ClusterResource — confirm status with Get-ClusterResource, then retry the update from Azure Portal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Update Issues/Azure Stack Edge upgrade failing due to Cluster Virtual Disk (ClusterPerformanceHistory) resource down

symptom: Adding VHD image to Azure Stack Edge via Azure Portal fails after 40-50 minutes with BlobDownloadFailed error
rootCause: The ASE portal VHD ingestion has a fixed retry count of 6. On low bandwidth networks (~20-30 Mbps), the download times out before completion, exhausting all retries and triggering BlobDownloadFailed exception
solution: Workaround: Use ARM templates or Azure PowerShell cmdlets instead of Portal to upload the VHD image (different transfer method). Alternatively, work with TA/PG to upload via robocopy from a PowerShell Support Session. Bug filed to make retry count configurable (ADO #14348396)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Virtual Machines/BlobDownloadFailed Error - Adding VHD Image times out after 40-50 minutes

symptom: VM deployment on Azure Stack Edge times out at last step with 'ProvisioningTimeOut' error
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: (1) Static IP assigned to VM already in use by Kubernetes host IPs — ASE can detect IPs used by other VMs but cannot detect IPs in use outside VMs (e.g., K8s hosts); (2) Incorrectly prepared VM image; (3) Default gateway or DNS unreachable by VM; (4) cloud-init failure during installation (Linux VMs); (5) Incorrect Provisioning flags in /etc/waagent.conf for custom Linux images
solution: Check for IP conflicts with Kubernetes host IPs and release/change conflicting IPs. Verify VM image is properly prepared. Ensure default gateway and DNS are reachable. For Linux: check cloud-init execution logs and verify waagent.conf provisioning flags. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databox-online/azure-stack-edge-gpu-troubleshoot-virtual-machine-provisioning#vm-provisioning-timeout
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Virtual Machines/ProvisioningTimeOut Error when deploying VM on Azure Stack Edge

symptom: VM on Azure Stack Edge fails to boot after disk resize — RDP connection fails with 'The operating system couldn't be loaded because a required file is missing or contains errors' (error 0xC0000098)
rootCause: File system corruption caused by the disk resize operation on Azure Stack Edge VM, leading to a non-boot state (STATUS_FILE_INVALID 0xC0000098)
solution: Connect via VM console to verify status (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databox-online/azure-stack-edge-gpu-connect-virtual-machine-console). Two options: (1) Create repair VM, attach corrupted disk, run commands to fix corruption, recreate original VM (no guarantee of success); (2) Rebuild VM from scratch and reinstall/reconfigure applications
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Virtual Machines/VM Fails to Boot Up after Resize

symptom: Windows Server VM on Azure Stack Edge stops unexpectedly on its own after running fine for approximately 180 days, showing 'stopped' state in Azure portal — may happen several times a day
rootCause: Windows Server trial license expiration. After 180 days, the wlms.exe process (Windows License Monitoring Service) initiates automated shutdown of the VM
solution: Extract EventLog to verify: 'Get-EventLog -LogName System > c:\system.txt', search for 'wlms.exe has initiated the shutdown' entries. Extend trial period using 'slmgr -rearm' command — can be extended up to 3 years in 180-day increments. Ref: https://sid-500.com/2017/08/08/windows-server-2016-evaluation-how-to-extend-the-trial-period/
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Virtual Machines/Windows Server VM Stopping on its Own

symptom: Cannot configure web proxy with NTLM authentication from Azure Stack Edge Local WebUI — the NTLM authentication option is no longer available in the UI after device reset
rootCause: NTLM proxy configuration was removed from the Azure Stack Edge Local UI. Local UI no longer supports configuring proxy with NTLM authentication type
solution: Configure NTLM proxy via PowerShell console support session using command: 'Set-HcsWebProxy -AuthType NTLM -ConnectionURI <proxy_uri> -UserName <username>'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Web Proxy/Cannot Configure Proxy with NTLM Authetnication from Local WebUI

symptom: Azure Stack Edge shows 'The web proxy URL is not valid' or 'Could not reach the web proxy server' error when configuring web proxy from Local Web UI — hcsmgmt_primary logs show 403 Forbidden
rootCause: Required URL patterns not fully whitelisted on customer's proxy server. ASE uses https://www.msftconnecttest.com/connecttest.txt for web proxy validation, and if this URL is blocked, proxy configuration fails with validation error
solution: Ensure all required URL patterns are whitelisted on the proxy per https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databox-online/azure-stack-edge-gpu-system-requirements#url-patterns-for-firewall-rules. Key URL: https://www.msftconnecttest.com/connecttest.txt. Test connectivity from ASE PowerShell support session using Test-NetConnection and 'Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing -Uri <url> -Proxy <proxy_uri>'. Check hcsmgmt_primary logs for specific errors
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Stack Edge/Web Proxy/Unable to Apply Web Proxy
